g.title: Non-skippable, even spikes get a worktree. Tracker persists for re-entry.
Spike is exploratory throwaway. Only cleanup runs to prevent debug prints/dead imports leaking when the spike gets copy-pasted.
Read past defaults/feedback from {MEMORY_DIR}/e2e/, recent git activity, and CLAUDE.md conventions.
Refresh the catalog of available skills. Avoids missing-skill surprises in later phases.
Fetch a GitHub issue by number and use its body + comments as the task description.
If unrelated work is uncommitted, ship it as its own PR first. Reviewers shouldn't have to mentally separate scopes.
Analyze raw prompt, identify gaps, output ready-to-paste optimized prompt. Advisory — never executes.
Generate a short PRD from a raw idea. Captures problem, requirements, success criteria.
Pick task type and stack tag. Drives skip-logic and stack-conditional skills downstream.
Build the in-memory task list of all active phases. Persisted to e2e-tracker.md in P4.
Built-in command. Renames session to e2e-{class}-{topic-slug} for findability.
Research-before-coding: find existing tools, libraries, patterns.
Context7 MCP. Fetch up-to-date framework/library docs.
Progressively refine retrieval — avoids dumping huge result sets into orchestrator context.
Pick the right layer: LangChain vs LangGraph vs Deep Agents.
Design exploration. Stop before step 9; orchestrator handles planning in P3.
Convene Architect/Skeptic/Pragmatist/Critic. Use for go/no-go calls with multiple valid paths.
Capture chosen approach + rejected alternatives as ADRs.
Always runs. Scan codebase for existing patterns related to the task.
File ownership, churn, recent incidents on affected files. Avoid re-breaking just-fixed code.
If change targets submodule asset (e.g. mobile-cursor-rules skill), FIRST read canonical path, not synced copy.
Decompose 5+ task plans before /writing-plans formalizes them.
Formalize task list with TDD steps + verification per task.
One engineer subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance, code quality).
Single-executor with review checkpoints. For tightly sequential plans.
Orchestrator implements directly. Forbidden when crossing service boundaries or touching prod.
Red-green-refactor. Bugfix: failing-test-first. Refactor: behavior-preserving.
Fan-out + result aggregation + failure isolation. Branch hygiene check before dispatch.
Root-cause investigation + Log→Reproduce→Fix cycle. Different failure shapes.
Persist decisions across tasks/sessions. For multi-chat work.
Debug artifacts, dead imports, AI-generated noise removal. ~30s, mechanical.
Reuse + quality + efficiency via 3 parallel subagents. Built-in command on Claude Code.
OWASP-style per-PR vulnerability scan. + belt-and-suspenders happy-path test for any defensive arg-mutation.
Coding standards, naming, immutability, structure. Read-only.
Perf/memory/efficiency optimization. Hot-path refactors.
Default for any HIGH or MEDIUM≥2. Tracking-as-mitigation is NOT resolution.
Package the review request (diff + gate findings).
Bugs and design issues over style. Constructive concrete feedback. Must-fix vs nice-to-have.
Classify findings. Push back vs comply. Don't be performatively defensive.
Triage which to apply, which to defer, which to discuss in-thread.
Hard rule: present actual output BEFORE marking AC complete.
grep + actionlint + unit tests are NOT sufficient. PR #106 missed 2 real bugs that empirical caught.
Test: capture snapshot → mutate upstream → call restore → assert byte-identical. Caught ckpm bug where restore advanced the lockfile.
~3-5min vs 15-30min full suite. CI catches the rest. NOT permitted for logic/schema/wire-contract changes.
Formalizes fail → fix → re-run loop with explicit stop condition.
User's commit hooks block any AI-attribution line. Strip it from default templates.
Present integration options as a structured numbered question.
Open PR + lifecycle (draft→ready→merge-strategy) + CI status checks + PR triage.
Release notes + deeper periodic security check (vs the per-PR /security-review).
Wire the Slack-canvas hook. Read channel context, draft message, ASK before writing.
Surfaced next /e2e in this repo, or noted in PR if hotfix is still under review.

text.tspan: code that ships to prod, not less)
